fields.description:
## Problem
The plan-review code-grounding finders (G1G2/E4/E6/G6) ground assertions ONLY against the origin/main snapshot and never see the ticket's depends_on prerequisites. A plan that legitimately relies on a symbol/file a prerequisite ticket will create is flagged "missing/unverifiable" and BLOCKED. G1G2 already SAYS "a symbol created by a ticket this ticket depends_on is treated as EXISTING" (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_G1G2.md:10`) but that rule is PROSE ONLY — unwired.

## Approach & rationale (G6/T1) — architecture-respecting, no new floor block
Introduce a machine-parseable inline citation `<subject> [rebar:<ticket-id>]` and honor it in two layers WITHOUT adding a blocking check to the DET floor (which is architecturally FAIL-OPEN: `det_floor.py` docstring — "blocks only on sound, unambiguous checks and fails open on everything else").
- LAYER 1 (deterministic, ADVISORY — genuinely mirrors `det_operator_attested`): a new `det_citation.py` reuses `det_operator_attested`'s inline-tag PARSING approach (both are advisory det detectors; `det_operator_attested` is imported as a MODULE at `det_floor.py:452` via `from . import det_operator_attested` and called as `det_operator_attested.ac_item_lines(...)` — NOT a regex-constant import). `det_citation` parses `[rebar:<id>]` tokens and, over the ticket's OWN deps already in context (`ctx.state["deps"]`), checks each cited id against the DIRECT upstream edges only (`depends_on` whose target is the id, or `blocks` whose source is the id). It emits an ADVISORY coaching finding for any citation lacking a direct edge ("add the depends_on/blocks edge or remove the citation") and NEVER blocks — respecting the fail-open floor. Pure over (plan_text, deps): no store traversal, no recursion (transitive is out of scope — cite your DIRECT prerequisite).
- LAYER 2 (agent — where blocking already lives): G1G2/E4/E6 are instructed that a symbol relied upon may be cited `<subject> [rebar:<id>]`; the finder treats it as EXISTING IFF (a) the citation has a verified DIRECT upstream edge (re-checkable from the deps in context) AND (b) `show_ticket(<id>)` (already in the reviewer tool set, `src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py` rebar_tools) confirms the cited ticket's `file_impact` contains the file that declares the symbol OR its plan/ACs name the symbol as a created artifact. Otherwise the finder grounds as normal — an uncited, edge-unbacked, or coverage-unconfirmed symbol is still treated as missing and BLOCKS via the existing agent criteria. Anti-bypass WITHOUT a new floor block: a fabricated citation earns no credit, so the normal missing-symbol block applies.

## Grammar (vocabulary — T8)
`<subject> [rebar:<ticket-id>]` where `<subject>` is free text naming the relied-upon code element (a file path, module, class, function, or config key); the TOKEN is the trailing bracket `[rebar:<ticket-id>]`; `<ticket-id>` matches rebar's canonical id or alias grammar. The subject is human-readable context; the bracket is what det_citation parses and the agent resolves.

## Grounding (E4/G1G2 — verified via Grep/Read)
- CONFIRMED `det_floor.py:452` = `from . import det_operator_attested`; it is ADVISORY ("p6 never blocks", `det_floor.py:486`). `det_citation` mirrors that advisory posture.
- CONFIRMED `PlanContext.state` carries the show_ticket state incl. `deps` (`det_floor.py:104-113`) — the direct-edge check is pure over `ctx.state["deps"]`.
- CONFIRMED `show_ticket` is always in the reviewer tool set (`src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py` rebar_tools).
- Rubric SoT: `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_{G1G2,E4,E6}.md`. `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is GENERATED (regenerate, never hand-edit).

## Scope
IN: `[rebar:<id>]` grammar; Layer-1 `det_citation.py` (advisory, direct-edge verify over ctx deps) wired into det_floor's advisory coverage; Layer-2 rubric edits (G1G2/E4/E6) + the show_ticket coverage protocol; author guidance; regenerate the criteria guide.
OUT (separate): T8 overlay over-fire (bug 1a05-e3a8); snapshot-pinning; transitive-closure citations; G6/coverage calibration.

## Acceptance criteria (observable + proving command)
- [ ] `det_citation.parse_citations(plan_text)` returns `(subject, ticket_id)` per `[rebar:<id>]`. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_parse`.
- [ ] `det_citation.unbacked_citations(citations, deps)` flags exactly those ids NOT a direct upstream edge (depends_on-target / blocks-source). PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_unbacked_detected`.
- [ ] The det check is ADVISORY — it never changes verdict to BLOCK (respects fail-open floor). PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_advisory_never_blocks`.
- [ ] G1G2/E4/E6 rubrics carry the `[rebar:<id>]` rule with the show_ticket coverage-check AND the explicit "uncited/edge-unbacked/coverage-unconfirmed still fail closed" clause. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_citation_rubric_contract.py`.
- [ ] Author guidance added. PROVE: `grep -q "\[rebar:" docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md`.
- [ ] Criteria guide regenerated + plan-review suite green. PROVE: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry regenerate-criteria-guide && pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review -k "citation or contract"`.

## Files likely touched
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_citation.py (new; advisory, mirrors det_operator_attested parsing)
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py (call det_citation in the advisory coverage lane; small addition — det_floor is 746/800 LOC, keep it thin)
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_{G1G2,E4,E6}.md
- docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md, docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md (regenerated)
- tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py (new), tests/unit/plan_review/test_citation_rubric_contract.py (new)

## Estimated scope: M
